title: rosa canina f. hirtescens
intro: Rosa canina f. hirtescens, commonly known as the scabious rose or hairy dog rose, is a variation of the Rosa canina species, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which can be attributed to its therapeutic actions such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in Rosa canina f. hirtescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Regular consumption of Rosa canina f. hirtescens has been associated with improved cardiovascular health and immune system function.
benefits: Rosa canina f. hirtescens has many benefits, such as being a rich source of vitamins A and C, potassium, and antioxidants that help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant's extracts have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as aid in the management of diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels. Additionally, the plant's antiseptic and antibacterial properties make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues, such as ulcers and diarrhea. By incorporating Rosa canina f. hirtescens into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health, enhanced energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.
actions: Rosa canina f. hirtescens has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. These properties also aid in reducing stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and boosting mood, allowing individuals to feel more balanced and focused throughout their daily lives. The plant's ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain also makes it beneficial for managing conditions like arthritis, fibromyalgia, and menstrual cramps, improving overall comfort and mobility. Furthermore, the antioxidants present in Rosa canina f. hirtescens help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, supporting healthy aging and immune function, enabling people to enjoy better physical and mental well-being.
intro_study: According to "Iranian journal of pharmaceutical research : IJPR", rosa canina f. hirtescens benefits from its enriched polyphenolic fraction's antioxidant activities, which were confirmed by DPPH, FRAP, ABTS, and nitric oxide assays, and its potential to inhibit H2O2 genotoxicity in human lymphocytes.
constituents: Rosa canina f. hirtescens has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (quercetin and kaempferol), phenolic acids (gallic acid and ellagic acid), and triterpenoids (ursolic acid and oleanolic acid), as well as vitamins and minerals like vitamin C and iron. These medicinal constituents possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and even reduce the risk of certain cancers. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Rosa canina f. hirtescens also exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for wound healing and preventing infections. By incorporating Rosa canina f. hirtescens into their daily lives, individuals can potentially experience improved overall health and well-being, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
parts: Rosa canina f. hirtescens has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, leaves, and hips, which are rich in vitamins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The flowers contain fragrances like geraniol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory properties, while the leaves contain tannins and salicylic acid, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The hips, or rose hips, are particularly high in vitamin C, flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, and pectin, which can be used to aid in digestion and boost the immune system. Additionally, the roots and stems of Rosa canina f. hirtescens contain sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
preparations: Rosa canina f. hirtescens has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are derived from its flowers, hips, and leaves. These preparations can be used for various medicinal purposes, including teas which are used to treat digestive issues and coughs due to their high content of vitamin C and flavonoids. The tinctures of Rosa canina f. hirtescens are often used as a natural remedy for wound healing and skin issues due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. Infusions of the plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their antiseptic and expectorant properties.
side_effects: Rosa canina f. hirtescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Ingesting excessive amounts of the plant's berries or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, the plant's high content of salicylic acid may cause stomach ulcers, bleeding, and kidney damage if not used under proper medical supervision. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Rosa canina f. hirtescens for medicinal purposes to avoid potential complications.
